title_en: Why Donald Trump won’t let a doomed bill die
title_zh: 为什么唐纳德·特朗普不会让一项注定失败的法案夭折
rubric_en: The SAVE Act is unlikely to pass Congress. Donald Trump may prefer it that way
rubric_zh: SAVE法案不太可能在国会通过，唐纳德·特朗普可能正好希望如此

paragraphs.en: AS THE MIDTERMS approach, Congress often becomes less productive. Members spend more time on the campaign trail, where they don’t want to have to defend controversial votes. The incentive to compromise diminishes, not that there was much to begin with. Yet this Congress recently passed a bipartisan bill aimed at lowering housing costs. In the coming months it is due to take up other measures, including annual defence legislation and government-funding bills.
paragraphs.zh: 随着中期选举临近，国会常常变得不那么高效。议员们把更多时间花在竞选活动上，因为在那里他们不想为有争议的表决负责。妥协的动力会下降，本来就不算强。尽管如此，这届国会最近还是通过了一项旨在降低住房成本的跨党派法案。未来几个月，它还将开始审议其他议题，包括年度国防立法和政府拨款法案。

paragraphs.en: But even that modest agenda has been thrown into doubt by Donald Trump’s fixation on a bill with little chance of becoming law. Mr Trump says he will not sign any legislation until the SAVE Act is approved. Prediction markets put its odds at less than one in ten. John Thune, the Republican leader in the Senate, has repeatedly told the president that the measure lacks the 60 votes needed to overcome a filibuster. “This is a distraction,” says a Republican aide. “We would like to be done with this, but it’s an issue that won’t go away.” Why, then, won’t the president let it?
paragraphs.zh: 但即便是这份不算宏大的议程，也被唐纳德·特朗普对一项几乎不可能成为法律的法案的执着推入了不确定状态。特朗普表示，除非SAVE法案获得通过，他不会签署任何立法。预测市场将其通过概率低于十分之一。参议院共和党领袖约翰·桑恩反复告诉总统，这项议案缺少通过阻挠议事规则所需的60票。"这只是个分心的议题，"一位共和党助手说，"我们希望尽快结束这个问题，但它却是一个总也消退不了的话题。"那为什么总统就不放它一马呢？

paragraphs.en: At its core the SAVE Act would require voters to provide proof of citizenship, such as a passport or birth certificate, when registering to vote. They would also have to present photo identification to cast a ballot. States would be required to compare voter-registration rolls with federal citizenship records. Supporters describe the measure as an election-integrity reform that would increase public confidence and ensure that only citizens vote. Yet evidence of voter fraud by non-citizens—or anyone else—is vanishingly rare.
paragraphs.zh: SAVE法案的核心内容是，选民在登记投票时必须出示公民身份证明，如护照或出生证明；在投票时还必须出示带照片的身份文件。各州也被要求将选民登记名单与联邦公民身份记录进行比对。支持者把该法案描述为一项增强选举诚信的改革，认为它能提升公众信心，并确保只有公民参与投票。然而，关于非公民——或任何人——实施选举舞弊的证据都极为罕见。

paragraphs.en: The SAVE Act would, however, make it harder for citizens to register and vote. Around 12% of registered voters could not produce documentary proof of citizenship on short notice, according to the Bipartisan Policy Centre, a think-tank. Roughly half of Americans do not have a valid passport. Democrats and Republicans are about equally apt to have at least one qualifying document. Yet Republicans rely more heavily on birth certificates, which are more likely than passports to be rejected because of discrepancies. The SAVE Act could therefore hurt Mr Trump’s own party more than Democrats.
paragraphs.zh: 然而，SAVE法案会让公民登记和投票变得更困难。根据双党政策中心这一智库的数据，大约12%的登记选民在短时间内无法出示公民身份书面证明。大约一半美国人没有有效护照。民主党人与共和党人在持有至少一份合格文件的比例上大致相当，但共和党人更依赖出生证明，而出生证明比护照更容易因细节不一致而被退回。因此，SAVE法案反而可能比民主党对特朗普自己的党派造成更大伤害。

paragraphs.en: For Mr Trump, the politics of the act may matter more than its practical effects. A recent Economist/YouGov poll found that 82% of Republicans thought voting by non-citizens in American elections was a serious issue, compared with 41% of independents and just 13% of Democrats. A majority of Americans (56%) support requiring documentary proof of citizenship to register to vote, but that view is held by almost all self-identified MAGA supporters (94%). “They love this, and so Trump’s going to continue talking about it,” says Douglas Heye, a Republican strategist. “And if Trump puts himself in a place where he’s fighting the leadership of his own party, his base loves that, too.”
paragraphs.zh: 对特朗普先生来说，这项法案的政治意义可能比其实际效果更重要。英国《经济学人》与YouGov的最新民调显示，82%的共和党人认为非公民参与美国选举投票是一个严重问题，而独立选民中这一比例为41%，民主党人仅为13%。多数美国人（56%）支持在登记投票时要求出示公民身份证明文件，但几乎所有自称为MAGA支持者的人都持此观点，比例高达94%。“他们喜欢这个议题，所以特朗普会继续谈下去，”共和党策略师Douglas Heye说。“而且如果特朗普把自己放在与党内领导层对立的位置，他的核心支持者也会喜欢这一点。”

paragraphs.en: In private, Republican aides concede that Mr Trump will probably use the bill’s failure as a pretext to blame lawmakers if the party loses seats in the midterms. Critics see a broader purpose. They argue that the SAVE Act is part of an effort to undermine trust in elections. “The strategy was never about necessarily passing this bill,” argues Alexandra Chandler of Protect Democracy, a non-profit. “It’s about setting the pretext to claim that if Democrats win, then the election can’t be trusted.” Mr Trump has repeatedly made such claims. Earlier this month his administration ordered the FBI to beef up an investigation related to the 2020 election in Georgia, reviving his baseless allegations of widespread fraud in the contest he lost.
paragraphs.zh: 在私下里，共和党助手承认，特朗普很可能会把该法案未能通过作为借口，如果共和党在中期选举中失去席位，就把责任推到立法者身上。批评者则认为其目的更为广泛。他们认为，《SAVE法案》是削弱公众对选举信任的一部分努力。“这项策略并不真的是为了一定要把法案推进，”非营利组织Protect Democracy的Alexandra Chandler指出，“而是在为一个说法做铺垫：如果民主党赢了，那么这场选举就不能被信任。”特朗普反复做过类似说法。上月初，他的政府要求联邦调查局加紧对2020年乔治亚州选举相关事项的调查，重新炒作他在那场自己失败的竞选中提出的、毫无根据的大规模舞弊说法。

paragraphs.en: Whatever the fate of the SAVE Act, Americans’ trust in the electoral process has already been deeply eroded, not least by Mr Trump’s repeated attacks on election integrity. Around 60% say they are confident votes will be counted accurately—a 17-point drop from two years ago. Only 25% say they are confident the midterms will not suffer interference.
paragraphs.zh: 无论《SAVE法案》最终走向如何，美国人对选举程序的信任已经严重受损，这在很大程度上都源于特朗普反复攻击选举完整性。大约60%的人表示他们有信心选票会被准确计数，这一数字比两年前低了17个百分点。只有25%的人表示有信心中期选举不会受到干扰。

paragraphs.en: Meanwhile, the work of Congress has stalled. On June 30th a group of Republicans in the House blocked a defence bill from advancing in an effort to force Senate action on the SAVE Act. A week earlier, inside one of the Capitol’s gilded halls, lawmakers from both parties milled around a stage, waiting for Mr Trump to sign the housing bill. But he was a no-show, refusing to do so until the SAVE Act was passed. To Mr Trump, “just about everything is a big yawn” by comparison. ■
paragraphs.zh: 与此同时，国会的立法工作陷入停滞。6月30日，众议院一组共和党议员阻挠一项国防法案继续推进，试图迫使参议院就《SAVE法案》采取行动。就在一周前，在国会大厦的一座镀金大厅内，来自两党的议员在舞台周围来回走动，等待特朗普签署住房法案。但他没有出现，拒绝在《SAVE法案》通过之前签字。对特朗普来说，“相比之下，几乎所有其他事情都只是平平无奇的一段小插曲。” ■

paragraphs.en: Stay on top of American politics with The US in brief, our daily newsletter with fast analysis of the most important political news, and Checks and Balance, a weekly note that examines the state of American democracy and the issues that matter to voters.
paragraphs.zh: 想紧跟美国政治，请订阅《The US in brief》——我们的每日简讯，快速解读最重大的政治新闻；以及《Checks and Balance》——每周专栏，梳理美国民主现状和选民关心的议题。
